items.title: Panthers confident of re-signing Nathan Cleary despite dad's departure
items.summary: Penrith Panthers' four-time premiership-winning coach Ivan Cleary announces he will step aside as coach of the NRL club at the end of the 2027 season.

items.title: What's in the federal budget for older Australians?

items.summary: Answers to all your questions about the federal budget's changes to negative gearing and the capital gains tax.

items.title: Adam Scott brings good form into 99th consecutive golf major at PGA Championship
items.summary: Adam Scott is being lauded for his "amazing" longevity as he prepares to tee up for a 99th consecutive major golf championship.

items.title: Farrer's Indigenous and migrant communities worried by rise of One Nation
items.summary: David Farley has won Farrer for One Nation in a landslide, but there are some in the community yet to be convinced of his ability to represent them.

items.title: Community mourns as Mornington Peninsula's Sammy the seal believed dead
items.summary: A beloved seal known as Sammy is believed to have died, after Zoos Victoria confirmed they were called to a rescue on Tuesday.

items.title: Gippsland man charged with threatening federal politician on social media
items.summary: A man will face court after federal police charged him following an investigation into a social media post that allegedly threatened a federal politician.

items.title: Five-minute solar eclipse in WA's north to attract thousands to 'middle of nowhere'
items.summary: One of the longest solar eclipses this century, albeit for five minutes, is set to put the remote East Kimberley on the world map.

items.title: Eurovision semi finals kick off with musical nod to Austria-Australia confusion
items.summary: The first semifinal of the Eurovision Song Contest includes a comedic musical interlude about the differences between Austria and Australia and sends 10 countries through to the grand final on Saturday.

items.title: New RSPCA facility at Pialligo among funding boosts for ACT out of 2026 federal budget
items.summary: Other funding boosts for the ACT revealed in the budget papers include $50 million to duplicate Drake Brockman Drive in West Belconnen, aimed at servicing the area's growing population, and almost $10 million for the National Film and Sound Archive.

items.title: Australia announces squad for Women's T20 Cricket World Cup, Darcie Brown left out

items.title: Goldfields women's shelter forced to adapt as abusers use tracking technology
items.summary: A women's shelter in regional Western Australia has had to adapt to an increase in technology-facilitated violence to keep victims safe.

items.title: Political fight brewing over budget tax reform as Coalition vows to fight Labor's changes
items.summary: The Coalition vows to do all it can to prevent property tax reform, which the shadow treasurer says will increase rents and make it harder for young people to buy a house.

items.title: Southampton beats Middlesbrough 2-1 in extra time to reach EFL Championship playoff final
items.summary: Southampton has defeated Middlesbrough to advance to the EFL Championship play-off final after a tense and heated two-leg semifinal.

items.title: Wil Anderson hates advertising. So why does he host a show about it?

items.title: MV Hondius passengers quarantine, fight disease as Dutch and UK governments order isolations
items.summary: The UK has ordered 10 people from remote island territories go into isolation, while parachuting in medical supplies, clinicians over suspected hantavirus case.

items.summary: Australian Test cricketers Travis Head and Pat Cummins have been part of a heavy loss in the Indian Premier League.

items.title: Georgia rugby players, including former captain, banned over drug test scheme
items.summary: Former Georgia captain Merab Sharikadze was one of six players handed lengthy bans from rugby for their part in a scheme involving alleged sample substitution and advance warnings of drug tests.

items.title: Former Carlton AFL coach Michael Voss says club in better place since he took over
items.summary: Michael Voss has struck a defiant tone after leaving Carlton, insistent the AFL club is in better shape than when he started as coach.

items.title: Commonwealth Bank shares slump 10.4pc in biggest one-day fall on record
items.summary: It was the worst day on the stock market for Australia's biggest bank since March 2020, when it dropped about 10 per cent, with sentiment also weighed by a trading update and broader weakness in bank stocks.

items.title: PM defends tax overhaul as Coalition accuses him of 'lying' — as it happened

items.title: Jannik Sinner equals record with Novak Djokovic for consecutive Masters 1000 match wins

items.title: Midnight Oil frontman Peter Garrett on 'closing the circle' to chair Landcare Australia
items.summary: The former frontman for the Australian rockers was among those who helped lobby for the formation of Landcare Australia in 1989, so it seems fitting he should circle back to chair the organisation after a lifetime lobbying for the environment.

items.title: Victorian emergency department nurse faces deportation due to son's condition

items.title: Native Pacific black ducks at risk in Tasmania due to introduced species

items.title: Big utes remain popular for Australians in March and April despite rising fuel costs

items.title: Will CGT and negative gearing budget changes make housing cheaper for first home buyers?
items.summary: There's no doubt that taking away negative gearing on existing properties will reduce the amount many investors can afford to pay, evening up the playing field for owner-occupiers. But these tax changes by themselves aren't a cure all.

items.summary: In 2024, Jayson Gillham's show was cancelled for his on-stage comments about Palestine. Now he brings a legal suit and a new recital tour with a Palestinian Jordanian artist.
